fragility_points: A broad crypto drawdown before Apr 19 could quickly put 80 back in play.
Binance-specific underperformance or operational issues could matter because settlement is exchange-specific.
key_assumptions: The market is mostly pricing ordinary short-horizon volatility around a current Binance SOL/USDT level already above 80.
Binance-specific settlement behavior remains operationally normal through the deadline.
No major crypto-wide downside shock arrives before Apr 19 noon ET.
main_logical_chain: Start from the live 88.5% market prior because SOL is already above 80 on the named settlement venue.
Verify the governing source directly: Polymarket rules name Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close as the source of truth.
Check current exchange-specific state: Binance public data shows SOL around 84.70, comfortably above the threshold.
Use independent context: CoinGecko recent range also sits above 80, supporting the market's bullish posture.
Discount modestly for remaining path risk because only the Apr 19 noon ET final close matters.
main_thesis: The market's high Yes price is broadly justified because Binance SOL/USDT is already comfortably above 80, but a modest discount remains because the contract resolves on one exact noon ET one-minute close on Apr 19 rather than on any prior touch.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
Direct Binance checks showed SOL/USDT around 84.70 with recent one-minute closes near 84.7.
source_quality_view: Source quality is good: direct contract rules plus direct Binance data and one independent contextual series. Source-of-truth ambiguity is low on mechanics, with modest operational ambiguity around UI-versus-API presentation.
strongest_disconfirmers: This is a future exact-close market, not a touch market, so a drop at the governing minute is enough for No.
Crypto can move several percent over a few days, so the current cushion above 80 is meaningful but not overwhelming.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance checks showed SOL/USDT around 84.70 with recent one-minute closes near 84.7.
Polymarket rules explicitly make Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET 1m close the governing source.
CoinGecko 48-hour context showed SOL trading roughly 82.96-87.34, keeping 80 below the recent observed range.
timing_relevance: The event resolves at the Binance SOL/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on 2026-04-19, so current-above-threshold evidence is helpful but not dispositive.
unresolved_ambiguities: Current evidence supports the venue and current level, but the governing settlement minute has not occurred yet.
The contract references the Binance trade UI while direct verification here used Binance public API endpoints rather than the rendered page.
what_would_change_view: I would move lower if Binance SOL/USDT fell back toward or below 80 or if a broad crypto risk-off move hit before settlement; I would move higher if Binance still showed a comfortable cushion above 80 closer to Apr 19 noon ET.
